topic: What music supervisors actually want — from 9 real briefs
cta: The supervisors who came back for Month 2 briefs came back because the first submission required zero follow-up.
caption_hook:
We've handled 9 briefs in 90 days.
Here's what every music supervisor actually wanted — from their words, not ours. ↓
caption_full:
We've handled 9 briefs in 90 days.
Here's what every music supervisor actually wanted — from their words, not ours. ↓

The supervisors who came back for Month 2 briefs came back →

#SyncMaster #AfricanComposers #SyncLicensing #MusicSupervisor

slides.body: Here's what every music supervisor actually wanted — from their words, not ours. ↓ — — — WHAT THEY ACTUALLY MEAN "Rights-cleared" means one-stop. — — — — — — — — —

— — — "Quick turnaround" means 24–48 hours, not 72.
The 11-hour turnaround in Month 2 got us the next three briefs.
Supervisors remember speed. WHAT THEY REMEMBER
WHAT THEY SAY THEY WANT "African sounds" means specific, not continental. WHAT THEY NEVER SAY BUT ALWAYS NEED HOW TO SPEAK THEIR LANGUAGE

"Rights-cleared tracks with stems."
"Quick turnaround."
"African sounds for diverse casting."
"Real African music, not fusion."

Every brief. Every time. Stems in the delivery.
Not "available on request." In the initial folder. Not five messages. Not "I'll send the stems tomorrow."

slides.headline: Open briefs. Every Tuesday.
slides.body: Composers: apply once, get matched for life. Supervisors: shortlist in 48h.
